title: Cryptool vs Repool: Running a Crypto Fund or Investment Club (2026)
author: Cryptool
excerpt_html: Repool helps you stand up the wrapper of a crypto fund: the legal entity, fund administration, and LP onboarding. Cryptool is the operating layer that crypto-native groups actually run on day to da...
content_html:
Repool helps you stand up the wrapper of a crypto fund: the legal entity, fund administration, and LP onboarding. Cryptool is the operating layer that crypto-native groups actually run on day to day: pooling capital, managing member allocations and vesting, distributing tokens on-chain, and reporting performance back to investors. Put simply, Repool helps set up the fund, and Cryptool runs the investing inside it.

At a glance

  • Core focus: Cryptool is an all-in-one platform for crypto funds, syndicates, and VCs. Repool focuses on fund formation and administration.
  • Pooling capital from members: Cryptool lets a group raise and pool capital with custom terms, tiers, and fees.
  • Allocation and vesting tracking: Cryptool tracks presale and IDO allocations, locks, and claim status per member.
  • On-chain distribution: Cryptool runs batch distributions across chains and handles refunds.
  • Group and member P&L: Cryptool reports ROI at the group and member level.
  • Secondary and deal sourcing: Cryptool adds an OTC marketplace and a deal database.

Where Cryptool wins

  • It runs the day-to-day, not just the setup. A fund wrapper gets you a legal entity. It does not pool capital across chains, calculate each member's allocation, distribute tokens on a vesting schedule, or show members their P&L. Cryptool does all of that.
  • It is crypto-native and non-custodial. Funds interact with smart contracts, with multi-wallet, multi-chain support built for how crypto groups actually operate.
  • Members see their position. Per-member allocation, claim history, and ROI are visible in the platform, which cuts the reporting work a fund owes its investors.

Where Repool is strong

Repool's strength is the formation and administration side: helping launch a fund vehicle, handling fund admin, and easing LP onboarding. If your priority is the legal and back-office wrapper and you will manage the actual investing elsewhere, that focus is valuable.

Which should you choose?

Choose Repool if your main need is forming the fund entity and its administration. Choose Cryptool if you want the operating platform a crypto group runs on: pooling capital, managing allocations and vesting, distributing tokens, and reporting to members. Many groups use a fund wrapper for structure and Cryptool to actually run the investing.

FAQ

Does Cryptool set up a legal fund entity?

No. Cryptool is the operating platform for running the investing. It manages members, allocations, distributions, and reporting. Use qualified counsel or a formation service for the legal entity.

Can members see their own performance?

Yes. Cryptool shows per-member allocations, claim history, and ROI, alongside group-level P&L.

Is it built for crypto specifically?

Yes. Cryptool is non-custodial and multi-wallet, with on-chain raise and invest modules built for crypto funds and syndicates.

See how Cryptool runs a crypto fund's day-to-day at cryptool.io.
